title: Redundancy only matters if you can reach it
author: Lee Briggs
summary: HP is integrating Tailscale into Remote System Controller, giving administrators secure access to out-of-band management devices through their existing tailnet.

title: Fixing my ridiculous fridge with a tiny Funnel site
author: Kevin Purdy
summary: I built a tiny transit dashboard for my fridge. Tailscale Funnel made it easy to share—and reminded me what belongs on the public internet.

title: Canada’s Bill C-22 and the security cost of collecting more data
author: Avery Pennarun
summary: Bill C-22 would push secure services to collect more data, retain more metadata, and build new access paths. That’s bad for privacy, and bad for security.

title: Introducing: Aperture CLI
author: Remy Guercio
summary: Aperture CLI makes AI coding-agent experiments easier.

title: Five TailscaleUp sessions I’d attend if I didn’t work here
author: Kevin Purdy
summary: I work at Tailscale, so I’m not neutral. But these are the five TailscaleUp sessions I’d still choose first.

title: Bambuddy: self-hosted 3D printing beyond the vendor cloud
author: Kevin Purdy
summary: Bambuddy helps 3D printer owners keep control local, while Tailscale makes that setup reachable from anywhere.

title: Fixing Headlamp OIDC login with Tailscale and tsidp
author: Alex Kretzschmar
summary: Use Tailscale identity to log in to Headlamp and reach Kubernetes RBAC.

title: How Cleric uses tsnet to securely automate software operations
author: Michael Saah
summary: By leveraging Tailscale and the tsnet library, Cleric built a secure connectivity layer that was easy for both customers and Cleric's operations.

title: Tailscale + Paperless-ngx: scan everything, expose nothing
author: Kevin Purdy
summary: Set up Paperless-ngx with Tailscale to store and securely access your tax, medical, and other documents, with optional AI tagging.

title: This month at Tailscale for April 2026
author: Kevin Purdy
summary: What's new in Tailscale clients this month, including Aperture features, Kubernetes improvements, and API-only tailnet access.

title: Aperture beta: better controls for the AI agent era
author: Remy Guercio
summary: Now available on all plans, Aperture offers better controls and visibility—just in time for the end of subsidized AI.

title: Meet tailscale-rs, our new Rust library preview
author: David Anderson
summary: An early preview of tailscale-rs is out now, and we’d like your help testing what comes next.

title: Pricing v4: more value, more simply
author: Avery Pennarun
summary: Tailscale’s updated plans are now live. The free Personal plan is more generous, business plans include more features, and pricing is easier to understand and predict.

title: Being the adult in the room
author: Avery Pennarun
summary: The industry is moving quickly. The hard part is not losing your footing.

title: The hidden costs of “good enough” network access
author: Jillian Murphy, Will Moore
summary: When infrastructure is simplified, the benefits scale across the entire organization: IT efficiency, worker productivity, reduced breach exposure, and serious ROI.

title: More Tailscale tricks for your jailbroken Kindle
author: Kevin Purdy
summary: Tailscale on jailbroken Kindles now supports proxies and SSH. And a plugin supports Kobos and Pocket Readers, too.

title: Aperture: Accelerate AI adoption without the lock-in
author: Remy Guercio
summary: Avoid AI lock-in with Aperture’s flexible, identity-aware AI stack
